title: 3 Unprofitable Stocks We Find Risky
content:
Running at a loss can be a red flag. Many of these businesses face mounting challenges as competition increases and funding becomes harder to secure.

A lack of profits can lead to trouble, but StockStory helps you identify the businesses that stand a chance of making it through. That said, here are three unprofitable companies to avoid and some better opportunities instead.

Magnachip (MX)

Trailing 12-Month GAAP Operating Margin: -18.4%

With its technology found in common consumer electronics such as TVs and smartphones, Magnachip Semiconductor (NYSE:MX) is a provider of analog and mixed-signal semiconductors.

Why Is MX Risky?

Annual sales declines of 18.8% for the past five years show its products and services struggled to connect with the market during this cycle Capital intensity has ramped up over the last five years as its free cash flow margin decreased by 42 percentage points Unfavorable liquidity position could lead to additional equity financing that dilutes shareholders

At $2.94 per share, Magnachip trades at 0.6x forward price-to-sales. To fully understand why you should be careful with MX, check out our full research report (it’s free).

EVgo (EVGO)

Trailing 12-Month GAAP Operating Margin: -28.8%

Created through a settlement between NRG Energy and the California Public Utilities Commission, EVgo (NASDAQ:EVGO) is a provider of electric vehicle charging solutions, operating fast charging stations across the United States.

Why Does EVGO Fall Short?

Poor expense management has led to operating margin losses Cash burn makes us question whether it can achieve sustainable long-term growth Limited cash reserves may force the company to seek unfavorable financing terms that could dilute shareholders

EVgo’s stock price of $1.99 implies a valuation ratio of 28.5x forward EV-to-EBITDA. Check out our free in-depth research report to learn more about why EVGO doesn’t pass our bar.

Cogent (CCOI)

Trailing 12-Month GAAP Operating Margin: -10.4%

Operating a massive network spanning 20,000 miles of fiber optic cable and connecting to over 3,200 buildings worldwide, Cogent Communications (NASDAQ:CCOI) provides high-speed Internet access, private network services, and data center colocation to businesses and bandwidth-intensive organizations across 54 countries.

Why Should You Dump CCOI?

Muted 1.8% annual revenue growth over the last two years shows its demand lagged behind its business services peers Diminishing returns on capital suggest its earlier profit pools are drying up Depletion of cash reserves could lead to a fundraising event that triggers shareholder dilution

Story Continues

Cogent is trading at $20.18 per share, or 9.8x forward EV-to-EBITDA. Read our free research report to see why you should think twice about including CCOI in your portfolio, it’s free.

Stocks We Like More

ONE MORE THING: Top 6 Stocks for This Week. This market is separating quality stocks from expensive ones fast. AI taking down whole sectors with no warning. In a rotation this fast, you need more than a list of good companies.

Our AI system flagged Palantir before it ran 1,662%. AppLovin before it ran 753%. Nvidia before it ran 1,178%. Each week it produces 6 new names that pass the same tests.  Get Our Top 6 Stocks for Free HERE.

Stocks that made our list in 2020 include now familiar names such as Nvidia (+1,326% between June 2020 and June 2025) as well as under-the-radar businesses like the once-micro-cap company Kadant (+351% five-year return). Find your next big winner with StockStory today.

title: Assessing Methanex (TSX:MX) Valuation After Strong Recent Share Price Momentum
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

Methanex overview and recent share performance

Methanex (TSX:MX) has drawn fresh attention after a period of strong share price moves. This has prompted investors to reassess how its current valuation lines up with recent returns and underlying business fundamentals.

See our latest analysis for Methanex.

At around CA$80.06, the recent share price pullback over the last week sits against a much stronger backdrop, with a 30 day share price return of 16.89% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 121.44% suggesting momentum has been building.

If Methanex’s recent move has you rethinking where the next opportunity might come from, it can be useful to compare it with other materials exposed names or adjacent themes using the 8 top copper producer stocks

With Methanex trading at CA$80.06 and an estimated 37% intrinsic discount, along with strong recent returns, the key question is whether the stock still appears undervalued or if the market is already incorporating expectations of future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 13% Overvalued

With Methanex closing at around CA$80.06 versus a most-followed fair value of CA$70.59, the current price sits above the narrative’s central estimate, which is built using an 8.08% discount rate and detailed earnings assumptions.

The analyst price target for Methanex has moved higher, with the fair value estimate rising from CA$62.42 to CA$70.59 as analysts factor in higher assumed profit margins and recent CA$5 to CA$12 price target increases across several firms, despite mixed rating changes.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what is driving that higher fair value even as some ratings cool? The narrative leans heavily on sharper profitability, disciplined discounting and an assertive earnings trajectory that is included in those cash flow forecasts.

Result: Fair Value of CA$70.59 (OVERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, that profitability focused narrative could be knocked off course if contracted gas supplies are disrupted or if the OCI acquisition proves harder to integrate than expected.

Find out about the key risks to this Methanex narrative.

Another view on valuation

While the most followed narrative has Methanex looking 13% overvalued at around CA$80.06 versus a fair value of CA$70.59, our DCF model points in the opposite direction. It indicates the shares trade about 37% below an estimated future cash flow value of CA$128.04. Which story feels closer to how you see the business playing out?

Story Continues

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.MX Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026
